text.div: intentionally left empty / not meaningful

tr.td: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Human isophane insulin 70 unit/mL and recombinant human regular insulin 30 unit/mL solution for injection
Inject 10 units (0.1 mL) subcutaneously before breakfast and before dinner
Amlodipine (as amlodipine besylate) 2.5 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (2.5 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (25 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (10 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (10 mg) by mouth once daily
Galantamine (as galantamine hydrobromide) 4 mg oral tablet
Prasugrel (as prasugrel hydrochloride) 10 mg oral tablet
Rosuvastatin (as rosuvastatin calcium) 40 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (100 mg) by mouth twice daily
Hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg and losartan potassium 100 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (25 mg hydrochlorothiazide and 100 mg losartan potassium) by mouth once daily
Clopidogrel (as clopidogrel bisulfate) 75 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (20 mg) by mouth once daily
1 spray (400 micrograms) under the tongue as needed for chest pain, may repeat every 5 minutes as needed, up to 3 sprays in 15 minutes
Metoprolol tartrate (as metoprolol succinate) 100 mg prolonged-release oral tablet
Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product
Clostridium tetani toxoid antigen adsorbed only vaccine product
Assessment of health and social care needs
Assessment using AUDIT-C (Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test - Consumption)
Depression screening using PHQ-9 (Patient Health Questionnaire 9) score
Development of individualized plan of care

ul.li: Support maintenance or slow decline of cognitive abilities in the context of Alzheimer's disease through dementia management interventions.
Promote independence in activities of daily living for as long as possible through dementia management strategies.
Reduce frequency and severity of behavioral and psychological symptoms associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Implement measures to reduce risk of harm related to cognitive impairment, such as fall prevention and medication safety.
Achieve LDL cholesterol below 70 mg/dL to reduce cardiovascular risk.
Maintain total cholesterol below 200 mg/dL.
Maintain HDL cholesterol above 50 mg/dL.
Maintain triglycerides below 150 mg/dL.
Patient will implement recommended lifestyle modifications to help control blood pressure, including dietary changes, increased physical activity, and medication adherence.
Maintain HbA1c at or below individualized target to reduce risk of diabetes complications.
Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg to reduce cardiovascular and renal risk.
Achieve and maintain a healthy body weight through diet and physical activity as part of diabetes self-management.
Engage in regular physical activity as tolerated to support glycemic control and overall health.
Follow a balanced diet with appropriate carbohydrate intake to support glycemic control and kidney health.

ul.li: Support maintenance or slow decline of cognitive abilities in the context of Alzheimer's disease through dementia management interventions.
Promote independence in activities of daily living for as long as possible through dementia management strategies.
Reduce frequency and severity of behavioral and psychological symptoms associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Implement measures to reduce risk of harm related to cognitive impairment, such as fall prevention and medication safety.
Achieve LDL cholesterol below 70 mg/dL to reduce cardiovascular risk.
Maintain total cholesterol below 200 mg/dL.
Maintain HDL cholesterol above 50 mg/dL.
Maintain triglycerides below 150 mg/dL.
Patient will implement recommended lifestyle modifications to help control blood pressure, including dietary changes, increased physical activity, and medication adherence.
Maintain HbA1c at or below individualized target to reduce risk of diabetes complications.
Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg to reduce cardiovascular and renal risk.
Achieve and maintain a healthy body weight through diet and physical activity as part of diabetes self-management.
Engage in regular physical activity as tolerated to support glycemic control and overall health.
Follow a balanced diet with appropriate carbohydrate intake to support glycemic control and kidney health.
